query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## US Strategic Petroleum Reserve — Current Level & Trajectory

**Current level:** The EIA weekly series (WCSSTUS1) shows the SPR at **319.5 million barrels** for the week ending July 3, 2026 (latest reading), and **325.7 million barrels** for month-end June (week ending June 26, 2026). This is the **lowest level since April 1983** — a ~43-year low, down ~20.7% year-over-year.

**Trajectory (past 3–6 months): Continuous releases/drawdowns, not fills.**
- Q1 2026 was essentially flat near ~415.4M bbl (small fills Jan–Feb, peaking mid-March).
- A sustained, **accelerating drawdown** began late March 2026, running at ~8–10 million bbl/week through May and June.
- Total decline of **~96 million barrels (~23% of the reserve)** from the mid-March peak to July 3.

**Driver:** A U.S. government initiative to release **172 million barrels** to offset a global supply gap following the "Iran war" (began end of February 2026) and to lower fuel prices, amplified by high crude exports and strong refining demand.

**Data note:** The FRED API did not return WCSSTUS1 ("series does not exist"), so I sourced directly from the EIA data navigator and cross-validated the numbers against YCharts, TradingEconomics, and BOE Report — all of which agree.

sources.snippet: Stocks fell by 6.2 million barrels in the week ending July 3 to 319.5 million barrels, lowest since April 1983; part of a US agreement to release 172 million barrels since the Iran war began at the end of February.

sources.title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
sources.snippet: Context on June 2026 refinery runs (17.1 million b/d, 96.1% utilization) and declining commercial inventories.

key_facts: SPR crude oil stocks (EIA WCSSTUS1) stood at 319.5 million barrels (319,489 thousand bbl) for the week ending July 3, 2026 — the latest available reading.
For month-end June 2026 (week ending June 26), the SPR was 325.7 million barrels (325,655 thousand bbl).
This is the lowest SPR level since April 1983, a roughly 43-year low.
The trajectory over the past 3-6 months has been continuous releases (drawdowns), not fills.
The SPR held flat near ~415.4 million barrels through Q1 2026 (peaking mid-March), then began a sustained, accelerating drawdown in late March.
Total decline was ~96 million barrels (~23% of the reserve) from the ~415.4M peak (mid-March) to 319.5M (July 3), over ~3.5 months.
Peak drawdown pace reached ~8-10 million barrels per week during May and June 2026.
The drawdown is part of a U.S. agreement to release 172 million barrels from the SPR to offset a global supply gap following the 'Iran war' (began end of February 2026) and to lower fuel prices.
Year-over-year, the SPR is down ~20.7% from ~402.8 million barrels one year earlier.
The FRED API did not return the WCSSTUS1 series ('series does not exist'); data was sourced directly from EIA and cross-validated with YCharts, TradingEconomics, and BOE Report.
